title: Netflix public replay similarity evaluation
summary: Compared the public Netflix landing experience between live site and generated replay output to identify missing assets, blocked navigation, and local parity gaps.
hypothesis: Public marketing pages can be replayed with useful visual and navigation parity if runtime asset bases and embedded image references are rewritten correctly.
notes: The evaluation improved asset completeness and image rewriting, but live-only dependencies such as recaptcha and telemetry still limited full parity.

title: Naver recursive replay fidelity hardening
summary: Re-ran a narrow multi-host recursive Naver crawl after host-aware paths, charset preservation, recovery-lane fetching, CSS fallback, and verifier fixes were implemented.
hypothesis: Replay fidelity on portal-style sites improves more from path, encoding, verifier, and asset-recovery hardening than from expanding crawl breadth.
notes: The clone now completes and verifies successfully, but many blocked external requests remain from adtech, telemetry, and remote widgets, and render-critical API mocking is still incomplete.

title: Malformed CSS regex fallback regression
summary: Added a regression fixture for an unclosed CSS block so clone output keeps rewriting imports and asset URLs instead of aborting the run.
hypothesis: Fail-soft CSS rewriting preserves replay value better than aborting the entire clone on malformed third-party stylesheets.
notes: Fallback stays scoped to imports and `url(...)` rewriting, which keeps the recovery bounded while allowing the run to finish.
